boundary_binding.action_boundary: May clinical operations activate the trial site?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: Trial-site activation is complete only when activation authority matches site, protocol version, consent version, activation date, and consent-status class before enrollment.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: Feasibility note FEAS-BOR-082 explicitly states consent status must be verified before enrollment, which is a critical condition not met by the activation authority document.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: A feasibility note remains visible; activation authority must prove abstract consent-status class before enrollment.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: The feasibility note (FEAS-BOR-082) states consent status must be verified before enrollment, creating an unresolved dependency.

final_answer: The trial site activation cannot proceed because a critical dependency remains unresolved. The feasibility note (FEAS-BOR-082) explicitly requires verification of consent status before enrollment. While the activation authority (AUTH-BOR-082) covers other aspects, it does not address this specific requirement, leaving the source boundary open and creating a trap. Therefore, escalation is necessary to resolve this abstract-field issue.
open_blockers: Unresolved dependency: Consent status verification required before enrollment.
